questionText: Read the sentence: 'The headmaster announced that the examination timetable would be released next week.' Which option correctly rewrites this sentence in direct speech?
options: The headmaster said: 'The examination timetable will be released next week.'
The headmaster said: 'The examination timetable would be released next week.'
The headmaster said: 'The examination timetable is released next week.'
The headmaster said: 'The examination timetable was released next week.'
explanation: En passant du discours indirect au direct, on rétablit le temps initial ('will' au lieu de 'would') et on supprime 'that'. L'option 0 est correcte.

questionText: Choose the correct relative pronoun to complete the sentence: 'The citizens, ______ voices were ignored, decided to organize a peaceful protest.'
explanation: 'Whose' indique la possession : les voix des citoyens. 'Who' est pour un sujet, 'whom' pour un complément, 'which' pour une chose.

questionText: Which sentence correctly uses the passive voice?
options: The curriculum committee will review the new program.
The new program was being reviewed by the curriculum committee.
The curriculum committee is reviewing the new program.
The new program has reviewed by the curriculum committee.
explanation: La voix passive se forme avec 'be' + participe passé. 'Was being reviewed' est correct. L'option 3 manque 'been' et l'option 0 est active.

questionText: If you ______ the vaccine, you ______ better protected against severe forms of the disease.
explanation: Il s'agit d'une condition de type 1 (réalité possible) : if + présent simple, will + base verbale. 'If you take, you will be' est correct.

options: He volunteered to gain experience for his CV.
He volunteered because gaining experience for his CV.
He volunteered despite gaining experience for his CV.
He volunteered so that gaining experience for his CV.
explanation: On exprime le but avec 'to + infinitif' ou 'in order to'. L'option 0 utilise 'to gain', ce qui est correct.

questionText: Read the job advertisement: 'We are looking for a motivated candidate with strong communication skills. Salary: 150,000 CFA/month. Send your CV and cover letter before March 10th.' What should the applicant not include in the application?
explanation: L'annonce demande un CV et une lettre de motivation, pas un acte de naissance. L'option 2 est donc ce qui n'est pas requis.

questionText: Which sentence uses the present perfect tense correctly to talk about experience?
options: I have applied for three jobs last month.
I applied for three jobs so far.
I have applied for three jobs already.
I am applying for three jobs since January.
explanation: Le present perfect (have + participe passé) exprime une expérience sans moment précis. 'Already' convient. L'option 0 utilise mal 'last month' (passé simple).

questionText: Transform into the causative form: 'We should ask someone to collect our garbage regularly.'
options: We should have our garbage collected regularly.
We should get regularly our garbage collect.
We should have collected our garbage regularly.
We should be collecting our garbage regularly.
explanation: Le causatif 'have + objet + participe passé' signifie 'faire faire quelque chose par quelqu'un'. 'Have our garbage collected' est correct.

questionText: Which modal expresses the most polite request?
options: Can you help me fill out this form?
Could you help me fill out this form?
May you help me fill out this form?
Must you help me fill out this form?
explanation: 'Could' est plus poli que 'can' pour une requête. 'May' est possible mais moins courant pour une demande d'action. 'Must' exprime une obligation.

questionText: Complete the sentence: 'Soil erosion is ______ faster than it is being formed, especially in deforested areas.'
explanation: Le présent continu (is + V-ing) décrit un processus en cours : 'is eroding'. L'option 1 est correcte.

questionText: Which word is not related to 'health prevention'?
explanation: 'Curriculum' relève de l'éducation, pas de la prévention santé. Les autres termes sont liés à la santé.

questionText: Choose the correct sentence using a modal of speculation about the future:
options: He must emigrate to find a job.
He may emigrate to find a better job.
He can emigrate to find a better job.
He should emigrate to find a better job.
explanation: 'May' exprime une possibilité (spéculation). 'Must' = certitude, 'can' = capacité, 'should' = conseil.

questionText: Read the text: 'In many cities, rubbish disposal is a serious problem. Toxic waste contaminates water sources. If citizens do not sort their garbage, recycling becomes impossible.' What is the main cause of the recycling problem according to the text?
explanation: Le texte dit explicitement : 'If citizens do not sort their garbage, recycling becomes impossible.' La cause est le tri non effectué.

questionText: Which linking word best expresses contrast in this sentence: '______ he studied hard, he failed the exam.'
Identify the error: 'The woman which works at the town hall helped me with my passport application.'
explanation: Pour une personne, on utilise 'who' (ou 'that'), pas 'which'. L'erreur est donc sur 'which'.
